title: Placement counceling session
content: Event Date: 16th June 2025 Event brief description The Placement Counselling Session organized by the School of Allied Health Sciences, Department of Physiotherapy, Galgotias University, on 16th June 2025, aimed to equip final-year BPT students with essential career skills. Held at the Old Auditorium, the session focused on preparing students for job placements through expert insights on resume building, interview techniques, and career pathways in physiotherapy. Renowned guest speakers, Dr. Rajesh Pal and Dr. Dheerja Babbar, shared their expertise on clinical expectations, global certifications, and bridging the gap between academics and real-world practice. Highlights included discussions on internship opportunities, portfolio creation, and specialized physiotherapy careers. The interactive format encouraged student engagement, with a lively Q&A session addressing queries on specialization, clinic setups, and career growth. Students appreciated the practical guidance and motivation provided, calling the session transformative. The event concluded with a felicitation ceremony and positive feedback from attendees, marking the session as a valuable initiative in supporting students' transition from academia to the professional world. Department Name – Department of Physiotherapy Event Outcome: The Placement Counselling Session proved to be a highly impactful and enriching experience for the final-year BPT students. All attendees reported gaining greater clarity on career options and felt more confident about entering the job market. The session successfully met its objectives by equipping students with practical tools such as resume drafting strategies, interview preparation techniques, and insights into securing internships and clinical placements. The expert talks by Dr. Rajesh Pal and Dr. Dheerja Babbar not only inspired students but also broadened their understanding of evolving opportunities in clinical, academic, and entrepreneurial streams within physiotherapy. The interactive Q&A segment allowed students to get their personalized doubts addressed, enhancing the session’s effectiveness. Post-event feedback indicated a strong demand for similar sessions in the future, especially ones focusing on specialized career tracks and skill certifications. Overall, the event strengthened the department's commitment to student development and career readiness, paving the way for better placement outcomes and professional growth
